articles.title: NHRC advisory to states as heatwave risks rise across India - EdexLive
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
CHENNAI: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) on Tuesday asked Tamil Nadu, Delhi, and 20 other states to take advance action and implement relief measures to protect vulnerable populations during heatwaves, citing rising risks from extreme temperatures.
In a communication to chief secretaries, the commission said the increasing frequency, duration and intensity of heatwaves disproportionately affect marginalised communities, economically weaker sections, outdoor workers and homeless people due to lack of adequate shelter and resources.
It said the elderly, children, infants and newborns are especially vulnerable to the health impacts of extreme heat. Heatwaves can also lead to livelihood losses and heighten the risk of fire accidents.
Besides Tamil Nadu, the states told to take preventive measures are Andhra Pradesh, Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Kerala, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Odisha, Punjab, Rajasthan, Sikkim, Telangana, Tripura, Uttar Pradesh and West Bengal.
Referring to National Crime Records Bureau data, NHRC noted that 3,712 people died in India due to heat or sunstroke between 2019 and 2023.
The commission urged states to ensure implementation of relief measures under existing standard operating procedures or guidelines issued by the National Disaster Management Authority to reduce casualties and mitigate the impact of heatwaves. It has also sought consolidated action taken reports from districts through the respective state and UT administrations.
Meanwhile, as temperatures soar across the state, the Director of Medical Education and Research (DME), Dr R Suganthy Rajakumari, instructed all the government medical colleges to set up special wards to treat heat related illnesses.
In Chennai, Government Omandurar Medical College Hospital in Omandurar Estate has set up a ward with 20 beds and Government Kilpauk Medical College Hospital has opened a ward with five beds, while Tirunelveli Medical College Hospital has set up a 11-bed ward and Coimbatore Medical College and Hospital has opened a 10-bed ward. Instructions have also been issued to other hospitals, including government headquarters hospitals.
Doctors said they were prepared to treat heat stroke cases, and electrolytes to treat dehydration were kept ready along with ventilators and drugs.
The Directorate of Public Health and Preventive Medicine recently instructed all primary and secondary hospitals to establish ORS centres in the respective facilities to avoid dehydration cases linked to heat related illnesses resulting in morbidity and mortality.
Meanwhile, the Food Safety Department has instructed food safety officers to inspect all canned water units in the state to ensure supply of safe drinking water.

articles.title: India Heatwave: Delivery Apps Ne Apne Gig Workers Ko Bachane Ki Race Lagai! - Whalesbook
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
Ab dekho, India mein jabardast garmi pad rahi hai, log pareshan hain. Is situation ko dekhte hue, Zomato, Amazon jaise bade delivery platforms ne apne gig workers ko is khatarnak garmi se bachane ke liye bade bade welfare schemes le kar aa gaye hain.
Amazon ne toh 'Project Ashray' launch kiya hai jisme 100 air-conditioned rest stops banaye gaye hain desh ke bade shehron mein. Yeh jagah saare delivery partners ke liye khuli hai, jahan woh thodi der aaram kar saken.
Zomato aur unki subsidiary Blinkit ne bhi kuch kam nahi kiya. Unhone 5,000 se zyada rest points organize kiye hain restaurants aur dusri jagahon ke saath milkar, jahan workers ko shade aur pani mil sake. Zomato toh apne riders ko ek comprehensive health insurance bhi de raha hai, jisme OPD, IPD sab cover hai.
Aur bhi suno! Swiggy aur Zomato ne innovative cooling vests bhi introduce kiye hain jo body temperature kam rakhte hain. Zepto ne apne dark stores ko coolers aur heat-reflective roof coatings se upgrade kiya hai, aur riders ke liye pani aur sanitary facilities ka bhi dhyan rakha hai. Bigbasket hydration products distribute kar raha hai, aur Flipkart ne toh 'Doctor on Call' service bhi shuru kar di hai delivery staff ke liye. Amazon bhi ambulance support aur health check-up camps laga raha hai. Basically, sector mein sabhi apne ground staff ka khayal rakhne ki race mein hain.
Ab sawal yeh hai ki yeh sab kyun ho raha hai? Toh bhai, ek toh competition hai talent ko pakad ke rakhne ki aur apni company ki reputation banane ki. Dusra, government aur public ki nazar bhi industries par hai. Zomato jaisi company jiska market cap ₹1.5-1.8 lakh crore hai, woh toh yeh sab manage kar sakti hai, par yeh sab karne mein paisa toh lag raha hai.
Par yeh jo welfare schemes hain na, yeh companies ke liye ek naya bada kharcha ban gaya hai. Jo companies pehle se hi thin margins par kaam kar rahi hain, unke liye yeh sab sambhalna mushkil ho raha hai. Yeh ek tarah se gig economy ki problems ka temporary solution lag raha hai, jahan workers ko kai baar kam pay aur uncertain jobs milte hain. Companies par pressure hai ki woh negative press aur regulatory action se bachein, isliye yeh mehnga race chalu hai. Agar koi piche reh gaya toh reputation kharab ho sakti hai aur workers bhi nahi milenge.
Aage chal kar lagta hai ki workers ki welfare aur bhi important hogi gig economy mein. Jo companies operational efficiency aur worker support dono ko balance kar paayengi, wohi aage badhengi aur talent ko attract kar paayengi. Ye ab sirph garmi mein help nahi hai, balki business ka ek important hissa banta ja raha hai.

articles.title: Government machinery put on alert following orange alert in State - Pioneer Edge
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
PIONEER EDGE NEWS SERVICE /Dehradun
The Uttarakhand government has put its machinery on alert after the meteorological centre issued an orange alert for several districts of the State on Sunday. The warning comes after strong winds, storms and rain affected many areas on Sunday with rough weather forecast on Monday. The Disaster Management and Rehabilitation secretary Vinod Kumar Suman held a meeting with all district officials to review the situation and issued necessary instructions.
Keeping the Char Dham Yatra in mind, special instructions were given regarding helicopter services. Suman said that helicopter flights should operate only if weather conditions are safe. In case of bad weather, flights should be stopped to avoid any risk to passengers. Officials said the meteorological centre has also issued an orange alert for Monday and Tuesday. All district administrations have been asked to remain fully prepared and take immediate action if needed. The officials were directed to share weather alerts quickly with the public through emergency operation centres so that people can stay safe and informed.
According to officials, an orange alert has been issued for Dehradun, Haridwar, Tehri, Pauri, Nainital, Udham Singh Nagar and Champawat districts for May 4. For May 5, the warning has been issued for Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh districts. During this period, there may be heavy rain coupled with lightning, hailstorms and strong winds blowing at speeds of 40 to 50 kilometres per hour.
Suman directed officials to reopen blocked roads on priority basis so that people do not face problems. Some roads were blocked due to bad weather, but many have already been cleared. Orders have been given to open the remaining roads as soon as possible.
